title: Teyana Taylor Sobs During Golden Globes Win for Supporting Actress: 'To My Brown Sisters… Our Voices Matter'
text: Teyana Taylor won the Golden Globe for Best Supporting Actress in a Motion Picture at the 83rd Golden Globes ceremony held at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills on January 11, 2026. She received the award for her role as Perfidia Beverly Hills in director Paul Thomas Anderson's black comedy action thriller One Battle After Another. During her acceptance speech, Taylor became emotional while addressing women of color. She stated: To my brown sisters and little brown girls watching tonight, our softness is not a liability. Our voices matter and our dreams deserve space. Taylor also acknowledged her children watching from upstairs and playfully thanked Anderson, calling him Paul let him cook Thomas Anderson for his vision and trust in her performance. One Battle After Another led the ceremony with nine nominations overall. Taylor's win came amid competition from Emily Blunt, Elle Fanning, Ariana Grande, Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas, and Amy Madigan.
